puzzle.title: The Hidden Conviction
puzzle.intro: In a time of tyranny and open defiance against divine guidance, a soul found themselves in a unique and perilous position. Living within the very stronghold of oppression, this individual held a truth that could cost everything, yet they chose to speak out, offering counsel and warnings to those who refused to see. Their words were a beacon of reason amidst a storm of arrogance, a quiet but powerful stand for justice and faith.
clues: I lived among those who wielded immense power, yet I saw their path leading to destruction.
I witnessed a great prophet's signs, but many around me denied them, calling them mere magic.
My true beliefs were kept secret for a time, for revealing them openly would have meant certain doom.
I tried to reason with a mighty ruler and his council, reminding them of past nations destroyed for their transgressions.
I warned my people against harming a messenger sent by Allah, asking them to consider the consequences of their actions.
I specifically mentioned the fate of the people of Nuh, 'Ad, and Thamud as examples for my own people to heed.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Qarun
Prophet Yunus
The Believer from the family of Pharaoh
The wife of Fir'awn
Luqman the Wise
identity.answer: The Believer from the family of Pharaoh

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Being swallowed by a whale
Engaging in a debate with a bird
Defending a prophet while concealing one's faith
Being thrown into a blazing fire
Leading a people out of bondage through a parted sea
trial.answer: Defending a prophet while concealing one's faith

location.label: Key Element
options: The Ark/Ship
The Staff that transformed into a serpent
The Mountain of Tur
The Kaaba
Pharaoh's court/council

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Being granted a vast kingdom and dominion over jinn
Divine protection and a warning to one's people
A flood that engulfed all disbelievers
Receiving a miraculous birth without a father
Being reunited with a lost family after years of separation
outcome.answer: Divine protection and a warning to one's people

puzzle.arabic: وَقَالَ رَجُلٌۭ مُّؤْمِنٌۭ مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَكْتُمُ إِيمَـٰنَهُۥٓ أَتَقْتُلُونَ رَجُلًا أَن يَقُولَ رَبِّىَ ٱللَّهُ وَقَدْ جَآءَكُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ كَـٰذِبًۭا فَعَلَيْهِ كَذِبُهُۥ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ صَادِقًۭا يُصِبْكُم بَعْضُ ٱلَّذِى يَعِدُكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌۭ كَذَّابٌۭ
puzzle.verse: And a believing man from the family of Pharaoh who concealed his faith said, "Do you kill a man [merely] because he says, 'My Lord is Allāh' while he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? And if he should be lying, then upon him is [the consequence of] his lie; but if he should be truthful, there will strike you some of what he promises you. Indeed, Allāh does not guide one who is a transgressor and a liar. (40:28)
